notes: G-open-question is the clean differentiator: with_skill BLOCKED (G3 fires), old_skill APPROVED — old skill never reads .swarm/questions/.

F-sibling-drift didn't differentiate: the brief's own prose ('same one leaf-04's cleanup function uses') forced both runs to adopt 1800. The sibling-read protocol is correct-by-construction here but the brief language pre-empted it. To exercise the protocol cleanly, future eval should use a brief where the coupling is implicit (shared field name) rather than explicit prose.

H-contract-proposal partial differentiator: with_skill wrote structured .swarm/proposals/leaf-02.md; old_skill blocked via narrative ASSUMPTIONS.md escalation triggered by brief's existing 'do_not_edit edit' stop condition. Both prevented the workaround. New skill wins on channel quality (structured vs prose) — graded equal under current assertions because assertions only check 'edited or not', not channel quality.

with_skill is FASTER (108.9s vs 143.8s mean). Counter-intuitive — explanation: the new protocol gives the leaf a clear single action ('write proposal, stop') instead of the old protocol's narrative escalation which required the leaf to also enumerate rejected workarounds.
Token usage with_skill is slightly higher (+2.7k mean) due to more skill text loaded. Acceptable trade-off.
